title: rottlera calcosa
intro: Rottlera calcosa, a tropical plant native to South Asia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the "himalayan stinkwood" due to its distinct unpleasant odor. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rottlera calcosa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in healing wounds, reducing pain, and preventing infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and treat various health ailments.
